items.title: Weak Passwords and Brute-Force Vulnerabilities
items.desc: Test for weak passwords and brute-force vulnerabilities.

items.title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
items.desc: Verify that multi-factor authentication (MFA) is properly implemented.

items.title: Password Recovery, Reset, and Update
items.desc: Check for password recovery, reset, and update vulnerabilities.

items.title: Session Management
items.desc: Assess session management, ensuring secure cookies, session timeout, and session fixation.

items.title: Logout Functionality
items.desc: Ensure proper logout functionality and invalidation of sessions.

items.title: Default Credentials
items.desc: Check if default credentials are changed or disabled.

items.title: Account Lockout Mechanism
items.desc: Verify the presence and effectiveness of account lockout mechanisms after multiple failed login attempts.

items.title: Secure Password Storage
items.desc: Ensure passwords are stored securely using strong hashing algorithms like bcrypt.

items.title: Token Expiration
items.desc: Verify that authentication tokens have appropriate expiration times.

items.title: Session Hijacking
items.desc: Test for vulnerabilities that could lead to session hijacking, such as missing Secure or HttpOnly flags on cookies.

items.title: Secure Login Forms
items.desc: Ensure that login forms are served over HTTPS and do not expose credentials in logs or URL parameters.

items.title: CAPTCHAs
items.desc: Verify the implementation of CAPTCHAs to prevent automated login attempts.

items.title: Password Complexity
items.desc: Ensure password policies enforce complexity requirements (length, character variety).

items.title: Remember Me
items.desc: Check the security of "Remember Me" functionality and ensure tokens expire appropriately.

items.title: Session Timeout
items.desc: Validate session timeout configurations to prevent extended idle sessions.

items.title: Inactive Account Handling
items.desc: Ensure that inactive accounts are disabled after a defined period.

items.title: Privilege Escalation
items.desc: Test for vertical and horizontal privilege escalation.

items.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
items.desc: Verify role-based access control (RBAC) implementation.

items.title: Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR)
items.desc: Check for Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR).

items.title: Access Control Policies
items.desc: Review and test access control policies for effectiveness.

items.title: Least Privilege Principle
items.desc: Ensure that users have the minimum level of access necessary to perform their functions.

items.title: Forceful Browsing
items.desc: Test if unauthorized users can access restricted resources by manipulating URLs or parameters.

items.title: Multi-Tenancy
items.desc: Verify that users from different tenants cannot access each other's data.

items.title: Security Misconfigurations
items.desc: Check for misconfigurations in access controls, such as overly permissive roles.

items.title: Time-Based Authorization
items.desc: Test for authorization that changes based on time or other factors.

items.title: Logical Access Controls
items.desc: Ensure that access control mechanisms are implemented logically throughout the application.

items.title: Role Misuse
items.desc: Check for roles being misused to gain unauthorized access.

items.title: Sensitive Functionality
items.desc: Ensure sensitive functionality is only accessible by authorized roles.

items.title: Dynamic Access Control
items.desc: Test for dynamic access control based on context (e.g., IP address, device).

items.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.desc: Test for XSS vulnerabilities (Reflected, Stored, DOM-based).

items.title: SQL Injection (SQLi) and NoSQL Injection
items.desc: Check for SQL Injection (SQLi) and NoSQL Injection.

items.title: Command Injection, LDAP Injection, and XML External Entities (XXE)
items.desc: Assess for Command Injection, LDAP Injection, and XML External Entities (XXE).

items.title: Client-Side and Server-Side Validation
items.desc: Validate input on both client-side and server-side.

items.title: Cross-Site Script Inclusion (XSSI)
items.desc: Test for the possibility of including external scripts that execute within the application's context.

items.title: HTTP Parameter Pollution
items.desc: Check if multiple parameters with the same name can be used to manipulate the application logic.

items.title: Remote Code Execution (RCE)
items.desc: Test for vulnerabilities that could lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Directory Traversal
items.desc: Check for directory traversal vulnerabilities that could allow access to unauthorized files.

items.title: HTTP Splitting/Smuggling
items.desc: Test for HTTP request splitting and smuggling vulnerabilities.

items.title: Path Traversal
items.desc: Test for vulnerabilities that allow attackers to traverse the directory structure of the server.

items.title: File Inclusion
items.desc: Check for Local File Inclusion (LFI) and Remote File Inclusion (RFI) vulnerabilities.

items.title: Template Injection
items.desc: Test for vulnerabilities in template engines that could lead to code execution.

items.title: Input Length
items.desc: Validate input length to prevent buffer overflow attacks.

items.title: Data Sanitization
items.desc: Ensure input data is properly sanitized before processing.

items.title: Encoding
items.desc: Verify that all input is appropriately encoded before outputting.

items.title: Logic Flaws and Bypasses
items.desc: Identify and test for logic flaws and bypasses in the application’s workflow.

items.title: Security Controls in Business Processes
items.desc: Verify the proper implementation of security controls in business processes.

items.title: Race Conditions
items.desc: Test for race conditions that could cause security issues.

items.title: Business Process Tampering
items.desc: Ensure that business process steps cannot be manipulated or skipped.

items.title: Transaction Manipulation
items.desc: Test for the ability to manipulate transactions to cause unintended outcomes.

items.title: Workflow Validation
items.desc: Verify that business workflows enforce proper sequencing and validation at each step.

items.title: Inconsistent State
items.desc: Test for conditions that could lead to an inconsistent state in the application.

items.title: Integrity Checks
items.desc: Ensure that integrity checks are implemented and cannot be bypassed.

items.title: Business Logic Abuse
items.desc: Identify potential abuse cases in the business logic.

items.title: Transaction Duplication
items.desc: Test for the possibility of duplicating transactions.

items.title: Conditional Logic
items.desc: Verify that conditional logic enforces the correct business rules.

items.title: Configuration of Servers, Databases, and Frameworks
items.desc: Check for improper configurations of web servers, databases, and frameworks.

items.title: Debug and Error Messages
items.desc: Verify that debug and error messages do not reveal sensitive information.

items.title: Removal of Unnecessary Features
items.desc: Ensure that unnecessary features, such as default accounts or sample files, are removed.

items.title: HTTP Headers
items.desc: Verify proper HTTP headers are set (e.g., Content-Type, Cache-Control).

items.title: Secure Directory Listings
items.desc: Ensure directory listings are disabled on the web server.

items.title: File Permissions
items.desc: Check for proper file permissions to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: Default Error Pages
items.desc: Ensure custom error pages are configured to prevent information disclosure.

items.title: Administrative Interfaces
items.desc: Verify that administrative interfaces are properly secured and not exposed to unauthorized users.

items.title: Third-Party Integrations
items.desc: Check for security configurations in third-party integrations and services.

items.title: Configuration Management
items.desc: Verify that configuration management practices are in place to maintain secure settings.

items.title: Software Updates
items.desc: Ensure all software components are up-to-date with the latest security patches.

items.title: Backup Configurations
items.desc: Check the security of backup configurations and processes.

items.title: API Security Settings
items.desc: Verify that APIs are securely configured to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: Encryption of Sensitive Data
items.desc: Ensure that sensitive data (PII, passwords, credit card information) is encrypted at rest and in transit.

items.title: Secure Protocols
items.desc: Verify the implementation of secure protocols (e.g., HTTPS, TLS).

items.title: Data Leakage
items.desc: Check for inadvertent data leakage in logs, error messages, or client-side code.

items.title: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS)
items.desc: Verify the use of HSTS to enforce secure connections.

items.title: Information Disclosure
items.desc: Test for information disclosure vulnerabilities that reveal sensitive information.

items.title: Backup Data
items.desc: Ensure that backup data is securely stored and encrypted.

items.title: Data Masking
items.desc: Verify that sensitive data is masked or obfuscated where appropriate.

items.title: Sensitive Data in URLs
items.desc: Check that sensitive data is not included in URLs or referrer headers.

items.title: Secure Data Handling
items.desc: Ensure secure handling of sensitive data throughout its lifecycle.

items.title: Encryption Key Management
items.desc: Verify secure management and storage of encryption keys.

items.title: Tokenization
items.desc: Ensure that tokenization is used where applicable for sensitive data.

items.title: CSRF Vulnerabilities
items.desc: Test for CSRF vulnerabilities and ensure the use of anti-CSRF tokens.

items.title: State-Changing Operations
items.desc: Validate that the application does not perform state-changing operations without proper authorization.

items.title: SameSite Cookies
items.desc: Ensure cookies are set with the SameSite attribute to prevent CSRF attacks.

items.title: Referer Header
items.desc: Verify the use of the Referer header to help prevent CSRF attacks.

items.title: Double Submit Cookie
items.desc: Check for the implementation of the double submit cookie pattern as an additional CSRF mitigation.

items.title: Custom Headers
items.desc: Ensure that custom headers are required for state-changing requests to prevent CSRF.

items.title: Form Token Validation
items.desc: Verify that form tokens are used and validated for all state-changing requests.

items.title: Secure Token Storage
items.desc: Ensure anti-CSRF tokens are stored securely and not exposed to attackers.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload and Malware Injection
items.desc: Assess file upload functionality for vulnerabilities like unrestricted file upload and malware injection.

items.title: File Type Validation and Storage
items.desc: Check for proper file type validation and storage.

items.title: File Execution
items.desc: Ensure that uploaded files cannot be executed on the server.

items.title: Content-Type Verification
items.desc: Verify that the content type of uploaded files matches the expected type.

items.title: File Size Limits
items.desc: Ensure file size limits are enforced to prevent denial of service attacks.

items.title: Virus Scanning
items.desc: Verify that uploaded files are scanned for viruses and malware.

items.title: Temporary Storage
items.desc: Check the security of temporary storage locations for uploaded files.

items.title: File Path Manipulation
items.desc: Ensure that file path manipulation is not possible through file uploads.

items.title: Storage Location
items.desc: Ensure uploaded files are stored in secure locations with appropriate access controls.

items.title: File Integrity
items.desc: Verify that file integrity checks are performed on uploaded files.

items.title: Sanitization of File Names
items.desc: Ensure that uploaded file names are sanitized to prevent directory traversal attacks.

items.title: Content Security Policy (CSP)
items.desc: Verify the implementation of Content Security Policy (CSP).

items.title: JavaScript Code and Third-Party Libraries
items.desc: Test for vulnerabilities in JavaScript code and third-party libraries.

items.title: Secure Use of Cookies
items.desc: Ensure secure use of cookies (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite attributes).

items.title: Local Storage and Session Storage
items.desc: Check for sensitive data stored insecurely in local storage or session storage.

items.title: JavaScript Obfuscation
items.desc: Ensure that sensitive business logic is not exposed in client-side JavaScript.

items.title: Clickjacking
items.desc: Test for clickjacking vulnerabilities and ensure the use of X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors.

items.title: DOM Manipulation
items.desc: Check for insecure DOM manipulation practices that could lead to vulnerabilities.

items.title: HTML Injection
items.desc: Test for HTML injection vulnerabilities that could compromise the application's integrity.

items.title: Secure Event Handling
items.desc: Ensure that event handlers are securely implemented to prevent exploitation.

items.title: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)
items.desc: Verify that CORS policies are correctly implemented to prevent unauthorized data access.

items.title: JavaScript Sandbox
items.desc: Ensure that any potentially unsafe JavaScript execution is contained within a sandbox environment.

items.title: Secure Frameworks
items.desc: Check for secure usage of client-side frameworks and libraries (e.g., React, Angular).

items.title: Client-Side Caching
items.desc: Validate that sensitive data is not inadvertently cached on the client-side.

items.title: Client-Side Encryption
items.desc: Ensure any sensitive data processed on the client-side is encrypted appropriately.

items.title: Presence and Configuration
items.desc: Verify the presence and correct configuration of security headers (e.g., X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Strict-Transport-Security).

items.title: Referrer Policy
items.desc: Ensure that the Referrer-Policy header is configured correctly to minimize information leakage.

items.title: Permissions Policy
items.desc: Check the Permissions-Policy header to control which features and APIs can be used in the browser.

items.title: Feature Policy
items.desc: Verify the implementation of Feature Policy to control the use of web features and APIs.

items.title: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)
items.desc: Ensure that CORS headers are properly configured to prevent unauthorized cross-origin requests.

items.title: Content-Security-Policy (CSP)
items.desc: Verify that CSP is properly configured to prevent XSS and data injection attacks.

items.title: X-Content-Type-Options
items.desc: Ensure that the X-Content-Type-Options header is set to prevent MIME-type sniffing.

items.title: X-XSS-Protection
items.desc: Verify that X-XSS-Protection is configured to prevent reflected XSS attacks.

items.title: X-Frame-Options
items.desc: Ensure that X-Frame-Options is set to prevent clickjacking.

items.title: Public-Key-Pins (HPKP)
items.desc: Check for the use of HTTP Public-Key-Pinning to mitigate man-in-the-middle attacks.

items.title: Rate Limiting and Throttling
items.desc: Test for API rate limiting and throttling.

items.title: Authentication and Authorization
items.desc: Assess for improper API authentication and authorization.

items.title: Data Exposure
items.desc: Check for data exposure through API responses.

items.title: Input Validation
items.desc: Ensure proper input validation and sanitization in API endpoints.

items.title: CORS Configuration
items.desc: Verify that Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) is properly configured to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: API Key Management
items.desc: Check for secure management and storage of API keys.

items.title: Versioning
items.desc: Ensure that API versioning is implemented to manage changes and deprecations securely.

items.title: Error Handling
items.desc: Verify that API error messages do not reveal sensitive information.

items.title: Parameter Tampering
items.desc: Test for vulnerabilities where API parameters can be tampered with to achieve unintended effects.

items.title: Mass Assignment
items.desc: Ensure that APIs do not accept and process unexpected parameters that could lead to mass assignment vulnerabilities.

items.title: JSON Web Token (JWT)
items.desc: Verify the secure implementation and storage of JWTs, including proper signing and expiration.

items.title: Input Whitelisting
items.desc: Ensure APIs use input whitelisting to accept only known and expected inputs.

items.title: Data Filtering
items.desc: Verify that sensitive data is properly filtered out of API responses.

items.title: API Gateway Security
items.desc: Check the security configurations of API gateways and their role in protecting backend services.

items.title: OAuth/OpenID Connect
items.desc: Verify secure implementation of OAuth and OpenID Connect for authentication and authorization.

items.title: Log Sensitive Actions
items.desc: Ensure that sensitive actions (e.g., login attempts, data modifications) are logged.

items.title: Log Integrity
items.desc: Verify that logs are protected from tampering and unauthorized access.

items.title: Centralized Logging
items.desc: Check for the implementation of centralized logging for comprehensive monitoring.

items.title: Real-Time Monitoring
items.desc: Ensure that real-time monitoring and alerting are in place for security incidents.

items.title: Log Retention Policies
items.desc: Verify that log retention policies comply with regulatory and business requirements.

items.title: Anomaly Detection
items.desc: Implement and verify anomaly detection to identify suspicious activities.

items.title: Security Event Management
items.desc: Ensure integration with Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems.

items.title: Compliance Logging
items.desc: Validate that logging complies with industry standards and regulations (e.g., PCI-DSS, GDPR).

items.title: Strong Encryption Algorithms
items.desc: Ensure the use of strong, industry-standard encryption algorithms (e.g., AES-256).

items.title: Secure Key Management
items.desc: Verify that encryption keys are managed and stored securely.

items.title: Certificate Management
items.desc: Check for the proper management and rotation of SSL/TLS certificates.

items.title: TLS Configuration
items.desc: Ensure that TLS is properly configured to prevent vulnerabilities like POODLE and BEAST.

items.title: End-to-End Encryption
items.desc: Verify the implementation of end-to-end encryption for sensitive data.

items.title: Random Number Generation
items.desc: Ensure that cryptographic functions use secure random number generation.

items.title: Deprecated Protocols
items.desc: Verify that deprecated protocols (e.g., SSLv3) are disabled.

items.title: HMAC
items.desc: Ensure the use of HMAC for integrity checks where applicable.

items.title: Secure Cloud Configuration
items.desc: Ensure that cloud resources are configured securely (e.g., storage buckets, virtual machines).

items.title: Identity and Access Management (IAM)
items.desc: Verify that IAM policies follow the principle of least privilege.

items.title: Data Encryption
items.desc: Ensure that data is encrypted at rest and in transit in the cloud.

items.title: Network Security
items.desc: Check the security configurations of cloud network components (e.g., security groups, firewalls).

items.title: Monitoring and Logging
items.desc: Verify that cloud monitoring and logging are in place and properly configured.

items.title: Backup and Recovery
items.desc: Ensure that backup and recovery processes are secure and regularly tested.

items.title: Serverless Security
items.desc: Verify the security configurations of serverless functions.

items.title: Cloud Compliance
items.desc: Ensure compliance with relevant regulations and standards in the cloud environment.

items.title: Vulnerability Management
items.desc: Ensure that third-party components are regularly checked for vulnerabilities.

items.title: License Compliance
items.desc: Verify that the use of third-party components complies with their licenses.

items.title: Secure Configuration
items.desc: Ensure that third-party components are securely configured.

items.title: Update Management
items.desc: Check that third-party components are kept up-to-date with the latest security patches.

items.title: Integrity Checks
items.desc: Verify the integrity of third-party components before use.

items.title: Secure Development Lifecycle
items.desc: Ensure that security is integrated into the development lifecycle.

items.title: Code Reviews
items.desc: Conduct regular code reviews to identify and mitigate security vulnerabilities.

items.title: Static and Dynamic Analysis
items.desc: Use static and dynamic analysis tools to identify security issues in the code.

items.title: Secure CI/CD Pipeline
items.desc: Verify that the CI/CD pipeline includes security checks and validations.

items.title: Deployment Security
items.desc: Ensure that the deployment process follows secure practices to prevent vulnerabilities.

items.title: Environment Segregation
items.desc: Verify the segregation of development, testing, and production environments.

items.title: Incident Response Plan
items.desc: Ensure there is an incident response plan in place.

items.title: Detection and Analysis
items.desc: Verify that incidents can be detected and analyzed promptly.

items.title: Containment and Eradication
items.desc: Ensure that procedures for containment and eradication of threats are in place.

items.title: Recovery and Post-Incident Activities
items.desc: Verify the recovery processes and post-incident review activities.
